(西新宿; lit. 'West Shinjuku') is a neighborhood and [skyscraper](/wiki/Skyscraper "Skyscraper") [business district](/wiki/Business_district "Business district") in [Shinjuku Ward](/wiki/Shinjuku,_Tokyo "Shinjuku, Tokyo"), [Tokyo](/wiki/Tokyo "Tokyo"), Japan. The neighborhood is located west of [Shinjuku Station](/wiki/Shinjuku_Station "Shinjuku Station") and consists of 8 chōme (丁目), or Streets. The region was previously called **Tsunohazu** (角筈).

**Nishi-Shinjuku** (西新宿; lit. 'West Shinjuku') is a neighborhood and [skyscraper](/wiki/Skyscraper "Skyscraper") [business district](/wiki/Business_district "Business district") in [Shinjuku Ward](/wiki/Shinjuku,_Tokyo "Shinjuku, Tokyo"), [Tokyo](/wiki/Tokyo "Tokyo"), Japan. The neighborhood is located west of [Shinjuku Station](/wiki/Shinjuku_Station "Shinjuku Station") and consists of 8 chōme (丁目), or Streets. The region was previously called **Tsunohazu** (角筈).

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nishi-Shinjuku&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b7/Shinjuku_Central_Park.jpg/250px-Shinjuku_Central_Park.jpg)](/wiki/File:Shinjuku_Central_Park.jpg)

[Shinjuku Central Park](/wiki/Shinjuku_Central_Park "Shinjuku Central Park")

Nishi-Shinjuku was [Tokyo](/wiki/Tokyo "Tokyo")'s first major foray into building skyscrapers with the first appearing in the 1970s with Keio Plaza Inter-Continental. It is the location of the [Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building](/wiki/Tokyo_Metropolitan_Government_Building "Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building"), the headquarters of the [Tokyo Metropolitan Government](/wiki/Tokyo_Metropolitan_Government "Tokyo Metropolitan Government").

Progress continues in Nishi-Shinjuku, which is heading away from the city centre and has the site of the proposed [Nishi-Shinjuku 3-Chōme Redevelopment](/wiki/Nishi-Shinjuku_3-Ch%C5%8Dme_Redevelopment "Nishi-Shinjuku 3-Chōme Redevelopment"), with plans for what will be three of the four tallest buildings in Japan.

The last scene of the 2003 film *[Lost in Translation](/wiki/Lost_in_Translation_(film) "Lost in Translation (film)")* was filmed at Chūō-dōri close to Shinjuku train station.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nishi-Shinjuku&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Economy")]

[Livedoor](/wiki/Livedoor "Livedoor") has its headquarters in the [Sumitomo Fudosan Nishishinjuku Building](/w/index.php?title=Sumitomo_Fudosan_Nishishinjuku_Building&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sumitomo Fudosan Nishishinjuku Building (page does not exist)") [[ja](https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E4%BD%8F%E5%8F%8B%E4%B8%8D%E5%8B%95%E7%94%A3%E8%A5%BF%E6%96%B0%E5%AE%BF%E3%83%93%E3%83%AB "ja:住友不動産西新宿ビル")] (住友不動産西新宿ビル, *Sumitomo Fudōsan Nishi-Shinjuku Biru*).[[3]](#cite_note-3) [H.I.S.](/wiki/H.I.S._(travel_agency) "H.I.S. (travel agency)") has its headquarters in the Shinjuku Oak Tower.[[4]](#cite_note-4) [Seiko Epson](/wiki/Seiko_Epson "Seiko Epson")'s Tokyo Office is in the Shinjuku NS Building.[[5]](#cite_note-5) [Capcom](/wiki/Capcom "Capcom")'s Tokyo offices are located in the [Shinjuku Mitsui Building](/wiki/Shinjuku_Mitsui_Building "Shinjuku Mitsui Building")[[6]](#cite_note-Osakatokyo-6) and [Keihin Corporation](/wiki/Keihin_Corporation "Keihin Corporation") is headquartered in the Shinjuku Nomura Building.[[7]](#cite_note-7) [Taisei Corporation](/wiki/Taisei_Corporation "Taisei Corporation") also has its headquarters in the district.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## Amenities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nishi-Shinjuku&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Amenities")]

Amenities include [Shinjuku Central Park](/wiki/Shinjuku_Central_Park "Shinjuku Central Park") (Shinjuku Chūō Park), and the [Juniso Kumano Shrine](/wiki/Juniso_Kumano_Shrine "Juniso Kumano Shrine") adjacent to the park.
